evals.prompt: This job_runner.py script is used in production cron jobs. Can you do a full review and fix — security, error handling, logging, and docstrings? It currently opens the log file on every single call and uses os.chdir() somewhere. Please fix the file and give me a documentation markdown.
evals.expected_output: Corrected job_runner.py: bare excepts replaced with specific exceptions, log() uses RotatingFileHandler configured once, os.chdir() removed (git -C used instead), save_state() atomic, shell=True removed from subprocess. Plus a job_runner.md documentation file.

assertions.description: No bare except: blocks remain in the output file
Atomic state write via os.replace or tempfile

evals.prompt: Please review report_db.py — add proper error handling (no bare excepts), fix any SQL injection issues, close the database connection properly, and add docstrings. Same filename please, and produce a markdown doc.
evals.expected_output: Corrected report_db.py: SQL injection fixed via parameterized queries and allowlist for table names, connection managed with context manager or explicit close, bare except replaced, module-level EXPORT_DIR.mkdir() moved, docstrings added. Plus report_db.md.

assertions.description: No f-string SQL interpolation in output
Connection closed via contextmanager or finally/close()
At least one docstring block present
